calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-16-2023 Off Calendar

Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing (Motions)
Off Calendar Nov-08-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar for Default Judgment Prove-Up hearing as to Defendant Albert Lee.
Dept.505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceeding recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:14:40 a.m., pausing at 9:18:15 a.m., commencing at 10:26:21 a.m., pausing at 10:37:57 a.m., commecing at 11:08:12 a.m. and concluding at 11:28:24 a.m.(D505)

David J. Streza, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff Janet Kim via Zoom Video Conference.
Plaintiff Janet Kim was placed under oath and testified via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Albert Lee not appearing.

Plaintiff's complaint for Damages for Motor Vehicle negligence; personal injury filed on April 29, 2020.
Proof of substituted service as to Defendant Albert Lee served May 24, 2020, mailed on May 25, 2020; service effectuated on June 4, 2020.
Default entered as to Defendant Albert Lee on March 25, 2021.
Dismissal filed as to Defendant Does 1 to 10 on August 21, 2023.
Amended notice and proof of service of Default judgment pro-up hearing packet served by mail as to Defendant on September 28, 2023; service effectuated on October 9, 2023.

The Court addresses Plaintiff's counsel and asks if they are ready to move forward because there are no exhibits or declarations filed with the Court.

Plaintiff's counsel to offer of proof, statements of fact and to summarize the case.

The Court asks which causes of action for damages are sought by the Plaintiff. Plaintiff's counsel emphasizes emotional distress and punitive damages.

Counsel examines witness for their testimony.

After hearing the testimonies, the Court takes the matter under submission.
